idea.title: Pet Vet Rush
idea.hook: Match each arriving pet to the right treatment station, keep the waiting room happy, and grow a bustling animal clinic.
idea.prototype: A Diner-Dash-style real-time clinic management sim, mobile-portrait-first. Cute pets arrive into a waiting room, each showing a CONDITION icon (broken bone, fever, fleas, vaccine, toothache, emergency). 2-tap assignment (NO drag): tap a waiting pet, then tap a FREE matching treatment station; it treats over a few seconds and pays cash (+tip if fast). Each waiting pet has a draining PATIENCE meter; an empty meter = the pet leaves angry and costs a CLINIC HEART (start 5). Clear the day's quota to reach a day-end summary, then spend cash on a deep upgrade tree (new stations, duplicate bays, assistants that auto-run a bay, treat dispensers, seats, speed). Each day adds a new condition/station, faster arrivals, and VIP/emergency pets. >=6 condition/station types, 6 upgrade kinds. Lose = hearts<=0 -> GAMEOVER.
idea.mutation_of: Diner Dash / Cook-Serve real-time time-management family (assign-customer-to-station, patience meters, day-by-day upgrade loop) themed to a cozy cute animal vet clinic
idea.why_novel: Our portfolio's only two simulation games are BOTH dating sims (date_the_look, starbound_hearts); the MANAGEMENT-sim sub-space is entirely unbuilt for us despite simulation being 100% liked and #2 by 7-day play seconds. Pairs the proven Diner-Dash assign->cure->upgrade loop (a high retention-ceiling genre) with a cozy-cute animal theme that targets the cozy_cute / weird_creature CTR archetype. 2-tap (no drag) assignment directly answers repeated player complaints that drag controls are bad.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Clinic hearts (lives) + per-pet patience meters + day quota (resource/lives scarcity). No global countdown timer anywhere; the only clock-like elements are per-pet patience rings and per-station progress bars, which are local feedback, not a loss timer.
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when hearts<=0 (every pet that runs out of patience and leaves angry costs one of 5 clinic hearts). Reachable from random/no-input play in <60s. Forgiving: a wrong-station misassign costs no heart, and RETRY restarts instantly.

sources.title: simulation genre = 100% liked, strong retention in the portfolio knowledge input
sources.snippet: Genre rollup (avg retention): simulation — 2 games · 94.0s/play avg · 5 plays · likes 2/0 (100%). One of the highest like-ratio genres we have; the knowledge input directs picking the strongest new game that extends BUILD-MORE winners / proven mechanics. Management-sim is an unbuilt extension of our liked simulation space.

sources.title: simulation is #2 by 7-day play seconds and 100% liked, but both sims are dating sims
sources.snippet: Per-genre 7-day rollup: simulation — 2 games · 470s play (7d) · likes 2/0 · ratio 100% (ranks #2 by total 7-day play seconds behind 'other'). Top games by 7d play seconds include date_the_look (simulation) 285s and starbound_hearts (simulation) 185s — our ONLY two sims, both dating sims. The management-sim sub-space is unbuilt. Guidance: bias toward genres with higher 7-day play seconds AND positive ratio.

sources.title: Simulation / time-management broadly present across distribution platforms
sources.snippet: Simulation and real-time time-management (Diner Dash / cooking / clinic management) are a perennial, broadly-present casual category across Google Play, Poki and CrazyGames. The assign-to-station + per-customer-patience + between-round-upgrade loop is the canonical, high-retention-ceiling structure for the family, validating a cozy-cute animal-clinic take for our unbuilt management-sim slot.

synthesizer_brief: Build ONE complete production-grade mobile-portrait-first vanilla-JS canvas time-management sim: a cute animal vet clinic. THE CATCH = an addictive 10-minute escalating-day core loop. Pets arrive with condition icons; 2-tap assign (NO drag) to the matching free station; cure for cash (+tip if fast); per-pet patience drains and empty = lose a clinic heart (5 hearts; hearts<=0 -> GAMEOVER, reachable <60s). Clear the day quota -> day-end summary -> spend cash on a DEEP upgrade tree (>=6 upgrade kinds). Escalate every day: +1 new condition/station through day 5 (>=6 types total), faster arrivals, bigger quotas, VIP/emergency pets mid-game, then deepen via duplicate bays + assistants + dispensers + speed so something new still appears at minute ~8. In-session day loop only; cross-session retention deferred to the iterate pass. Cozy_cute CTR cover (adorable bandaged pet close-up, no title text). Wire GF.exposeState (all DESIGN vars) + GF.exposeBot (assign/buy/next_day) for the progression probe.

alternatives.title: Crystal Cavern
alternatives.hook: Dig and match glowing crystal veins down an endless cavern, chaining colors for combo blasts before the rock crushes in.
alternatives.why_novel: vertical encroaching-rock pressure replaces a move/timer as the non-timer threat

alternatives.title: Ricochet Clinic
alternatives.hook: Bank a single bouncing pill off walls to hit every germ on the board in one aimed shot per turn.
alternatives.why_novel: one-shot-per-turn aiming with a medical theme; ammo scarcity is the pressure

alternatives.title: Chess Blitz Mayhem
alternatives.hook: Capture waves of incoming enemy pieces using real chess moves, but every move spawns more attackers.
alternatives.why_novel: turns chess rules into a wave-survival juggle with a move-budget non-timer squeeze

alternatives.title: Stunt Constructor
alternatives.hook: Snap together a ramp run from limited parts, then launch a car to nail the target jump and land clean.
alternatives.why_novel: parts-budget scarcity + a build phase then a one-shot launch, no countdown

alternatives.title: Idle Sushi Conveyor
alternatives.hook: Tap plates off a conveyor for coins; upgrade belt speed and chefs; unlock premium rolls that multiply income.
alternatives.why_novel: conveyor motion gives idle income a physical read; adjacent to our just-shipped idle_noodle_bar so deprioritised this run
